# Rimsky

> Project-agnostic reactive node-graph orchestration platform. Workflows are graphs of nodes; an `invalidate` propagates through the graph and the scheduler recalculates eligible stale nodes. Coding agents are the primary documentation consumer.

This is a self-contained, agent-facing corpus: every link below resolves within these docs (paths are relative to the corpus root). It is the same surface the Claude Code skill router (`SKILL.md`) covers — concepts, protocol-implementation guides, generated references, worked configs, the cookbook, system-design patterns, the bundled-service and image catalogs, deployment, and the error catalog. The rimsky release this corpus is reconciled against is recorded in the plugin manifest (`.claude-plugin/plugin.json`, `reconciledAgainst`).

## Concepts

- [Concept catalog (glossary)](glossary.md): the full table of contents over every concept noun rimsky traffics in. Each entry names a concept; the per-concept files in `concepts/<slug>.md` carry its definition, purpose, boundaries, and invariants. Read the catalog first to learn the vocabulary, then the specific concept files load-bearing for your task.

## Protocols

How to implement a service that speaks one of rimsky's wire protocols. The guides are the practical "how to build this" narrative; the field-level wire shapes live in the generated reference under `protocols/reference/`.

- [Protocols overview](protocols/README.md): the HTTP+JSON encoding convention, the optional `@rimsky-ai/protocols` npm package and Go helper packages, and links to the generated wire reference (`protocols/reference/`).
- [ClaimProducer](protocols/claim-producer.md): implement a claim producer (Open / Commit / Abandon / Release / Capabilities).
- [Executor](protocols/executor.md): implement an executor — `Executor.Execute` (required dispatch protocol) and the optional `ExecutorObservability` protocol.
- [LifecycleSubscriber](protocols/lifecycle-subscriber.md): implement a lifecycle subscriber (seven hooks — six template/instance state-transition hooks plus `OnRunScopeTerminal`).
- [Publisher](protocols/publisher.md): implement a publisher (Capabilities / Subscribe / Unsubscribe / ListSubscriptions; emit via `POST /instances/{id}/messages`). Sensors (cron / http / object-store / webhook) are one kind of publisher.
- [Go packages](protocols/go-packages.md): the optional Go helper packages in the single `protocols` module (conveniences — not a required SDK).
- [Runnable examples](examples/README.md): minimal, copyable Apache server skeletons — executor, claim-producer, atomic-staging-fs-producer, lifecycle-subscriber, publisher, validation, data-processing — vendored from rimsky-core's `examples/` module at the reconciled tag, each with the exact Go wiring and a pinned `go.mod`; plus the `examples/compose/` directory carrying a runnable `rimsky-compose.yml` manifest and two referenced `TemplateSpec` files (`template-a.yml`, `template-b.yml`) for the `rimsky compose up | plan | down` verbs.

## Reference

The definitive specifics an agent reads to build a template or deployment plan.

- [Template & `rimsky.yml` schema](reference/template-schema.md): the complete template / config schema.
- [REST API](reference/rest-api.md): every control-API route, method, and per-action auth gate.
- [CLI](reference/cli.md): the `rimsky` command surface. Covers `rimsky compose` (`up | down | plan | status`) — the declarative reconcile verb that owns the reserved `compose:<project>:` tag / instance-key namespace and operates against an already-running rimsky (it starts nothing).
- [Worked configs](reference/config/rimsky.yml): a copyable unified `rimsky.yml`; the same directory holds the all-in-one, store, and supervisor examples.

## Cookbook

- [Cookbook](cookbook/README.md): the canonical patterns rimsky's primitives span — queue worker, reactive recompute, fan-in, capacity limit, convergence loop, event-driven node, claim handoff, sub-graph, local-binary executor (dev-loop dispatch through the host-agent proxy). Each is problem → rimsky shape → copyable template → gotchas → without-rimsky.

## Patterns

Higher-altitude system shapes (with explicit shipped-vs-aspirational status).

- [Domain stores](patterns/domain-stores.md): giving an agent project-specific state.
- [Operational health](patterns/operational-health.md): diagnostics, metrics, and the lifecycle-event surface.

## Bundled services & images

- [Services](services/README.md): the bundled stores, executors, sensors, and subscribers — config keys, ports, protocols, and the Dockerfile that builds each.
- [Images](images/README.md): the published image set and the `rimsky` entrypoint's role selection.
- [Stores (stub)](stores/stub/README.md) · [Executors (stub)](executors/stub/README.md) · [Blob backends](blob-backends/README.md) · [MCP servers (control-api)](mcp-servers/control-api/README.md): the building-block catalogs and reference implementations.

## Deployment & operations

- [Operator guide](operator-guide.md): deploy from the published images, configure (instance lifecycle, retention, parked-duration caps, the validating entrypoint), and diagnose.

## Examples

- [Minimal rimsky.yml](agents/examples/minimal-rimsky-yml.md)
- [Minimal template and instance](agents/examples/minimal-template-and-instance.md)
- [Two-node template with a claim dependency](agents/examples/two-node-with-claim.md)
- [Template demonstrating attribute-default inertness](agents/examples/claude-agent-attribute-defaults.md)
- [Holding-subgraph template demonstrating held-claim resolution](agents/examples/holding-subgraph.md)

## Errors

- [Error catalog](agents/errors/README.md): one file per consumer-observable error — what it means, when it happens, what to do.

## Optional

- [README](README.md): corpus orientation and how the pieces fit.
- [Comparison](comparison.md): rimsky versus alternatives.
- [Licensing](licensing.md): the Apache-island / dual-licensed-orchestrator posture.
- [llms-full.txt](agents/llms-full.txt): the concatenated full text (concept bodies + protocol guides) for single-file ingestion.
